what_it_does: This file is a posted noticeboard note in which Tim records a brief point about a proposed comparative research task on collaborative-annotation web products, agreeing it could be useful while noting the eventual application would be custom.
observations: The item is a note with state 'posted' authored by tim in the dragnet-development channel.
It links to board://item-b40afa03 with kind 'commented_on'.

The body is a short [point] response to a research proposal covering Google Docs, Figma, Medium, Hypothesis, GitHub review, and Linear across anchoring, threading, batching, and mobile patterns plus failure modes.
Created and updated timestamps are identical, indicating an initial filing with no later edits.
standouts: Explicit list of six reference products and four comparison dimensions (anchoring, threading, batching, mobile).
The author takes a pragmatic, qualified stance: the research is useful but the product will be custom rather than a clone of any existing tool.
It is a threaded comment on another board item rather than a standalone proposal.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, thread, links, created/updated, and history fields.
Follows the noticeboard item schema: type 'note', state 'posted', link kind 'commented_on'.
Body uses a [point] tag and quotes the referenced item title inline.
Timestamps are ISO 8601 with timezone offset.
concerns: The body is terse and does not record a concrete decision, next step, or acceptance criteria.
The title 'Tim · point' is author-centric rather than content-centric, making discovery harder.
The 'thread' field is empty, so the relationship to other comments on the same parent is not explicit.
notes: A short stakeholder comment endorsing competitive UX research for a custom collaborative-annotation product while cautioning against copying any single existing tool.
questions: What is the content of item-b40afa03 and the exact research scope it proposed?
What custom collaborative-annotation application is being designed?
Will this research be commissioned, and if so, who is responsible and what deliverables are expected?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to post a point/comment, which matches the file's actual content.
apparent_themes: Anchoring, threading, batching, and mobile interaction patterns
intention_signals: The author seems to be trying to validate the idea of benchmarking existing collaborative-annotation tools while steering the team away from assuming the new product will map directly to any one of them.
novelty: The framing of a 'collaborative-annotation product researcher' task that explicitly compares anchoring, threading, batching, and mobile behaviors across six distinct products including failure modes.
contribution: Captures a concise stakeholder position that competitive UX research is valuable but the target product will be custom, along with a concrete set of reference products and evaluation dimensions to include.
summary_for_embedding: A posted noticeboard note by tim in dragnet-development commenting on a research proposal for a web collaborative-annotation product, listing Google Docs, Figma, Medium, Hypothesis, GitHub review, and Linear as references for anchoring, threading, batching, and mobile patterns and failure modes, and noting the final product will be custom.
intention_for_embedding: To record a qualified agreement that benchmarking existing collaborative-annotation tools could be useful while clarifying that the product being built will not simply be one of those tools.
